intended_use: The AiORTA - Plan tool is an image analysis software tool for volumetric assessment. It provides volumetric visualization and measurements based on 3D reconstruction computed from cardiovascular CTA s
intended_use_full: The AiORTA - Plan tool is an image analysis software tool for volumetric assessment. It provides volumetric visualization and measurements based on 3D reconstruction computed from cardiovascular CTA scans. The software device is intended to provide adjunct information to a licensed healthcare practitioner (HCP) in addition to clinical data and other inputs, as a measurement tool used in assessment of aortic aneurysm, pre-operative evaluation, planning and sizing for cardiovascular intervention and surgery, and for post-operative evaluation in patients 22 years old and older. The device is not intended to provide stand-alone diagnosis or suggest an immediate course of action in treatment or patient management.
target_condition: aortic aneurysm; cardiovascular intervention and surgery planning
ground_truth: Annotations approved by 3 US-based board-certified Radiologists; comparison to Simpleware ScanIP Medical reference device for volume measurements
other_metrics: Auto-segmentation: Mean Dice coefficient 89% for aortic wall and 89% for aortic lumen (acceptance criterion ≥80%); Landmark identification: Celiac artery 2.47mm mean distance, Renal arteries 3.51mm mean distance (criterion ≤5mm); Length measurements: Mean absolute error 5.3-7.0mm (criterion ≤6.0mm for most, one exceeded); Diameter measurements: Mean absolute error 1.9-2.5mm (criterion ≤2.3mm); Volume: Mean absolute error 0.00242 mL for wall and 0.00257 mL for lumen (criterion ≤1.8 mL); Device-expert MPAD vs Expert-expert MPAD: Length (renal to left iliac) 6.9mm vs 7.1mm, Length (renal to right iliac) 9.6mm vs 10.4mm, Diameter (wall right iliac) 2.5mm vs 2.7mm
